learning_outcomes.description: Evaluate the nutritional composition and functional characteristics of feed ingredients and their contribution to meeting animal nutrient requirements.
Analyse digestive physiology, nutrient digestion and absorption, and compare digestive and metabolic strategies of monogastric and ruminant animals in relation to feed utilisation.
Assess nutrient interactions and their effects on nutrient availability, metabolism, animal performance, and health.
Formulate and evaluate nutritionally balanced diets using feed composition data, animal nutrient requirement standards, and evidence-based feeding strategies.
Apply laboratory and experimental techniques to feed evaluation, including sample preparation, analytical procedures, data interpretation, and effective scientific communication.
Critically evaluate contemporary challenges and innovations in animal nutrition, applying evidence-based reasoning to inform decisions in research and industry contexts.

assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration
assessments.task_description:
Practical assessments include a series of practical worksheets and reports completed during the semester. The Practical assessments are worksheets and calculations done at the end of each practical class on a weekly basis (starting from week 3 - ending on week 12; for more details see Learning activities) including:

Prac assessment 1) - Feed ingredient, nutrient composition, sampling and proximate analysis, weight 5% - assesses learning outcome 1 & 5.

Prac assessment 2) - Digestibility of feeds, weight 5% - assesses learning outcome 2 & 6.

Prac assessment 3) - Chicken growth bioassay, conducted across 3 weeks (7 - 9), weight 10% - assesses learning outcome 2, 5, & 7.

Prac assessment 4) - Feed optimization, weight 5% - assesses learning outcome 1, & 5.

These assessment tasks are to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To obtain a mark for the practical classes students must participate and complete the associated exercises (submitting the practical report via Blackboard - Turnitin). If you do not participate in a practical class, or do not provide evidence of your attendance (i.e. signing attendance sheet, scanning your student ID card on entry to the practical), your report will not be marked/graded. Refer to late submission notes for more information. 

Absence from a Practical/Field Trip Session

If you do not complete the practical/field trip session, your work will not be marked for that session - this course is unable to provide an alternative practical session to attend.

If you are unable to complete your timetabled practical session because of circumstances beyond your control (e.g., illness) you must submit a practical absence request immediately accompanied by acceptable supporting documentation.

 A list of acceptable reasons and required supporting documentation is found at https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/applying-assessment-extension?p=1#1  

Your request must be submitted no later than 24 hours following the missed practical/field trip. The submission link can be found in the Learning Resources (Blackboard Original) or Course Resources (Blackboard Ultra) folder in your course Blackboard site.

Requests without the correct supporting documentation may not be approved. If the reason for your absence is not approved, you will be considered as absent from that practical session.

It is your responsibility to submit an application and provide correct documentation. If approved, alternative assessment will be organised with the individual student.

If you require further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact the School - agriculture@enquire.uq.edu.au.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The calculations and answers to the questions will be checked by tutors in the practical class.

assessments.category: Creative Production/ Exhibition, Presentation, Project, Role play/ Simulation
assessments.task_description:
Task Description:

Students will complete a video blog (vlog). This task requires communication of the relationship between the structure and the chemical/physical properties of a nutrient/food component and highlighting its role and importance in animal nutrition contexts. Students must choose one nutrient/compound from the approved list, which are relevant to animal nutrition contexts. Further information and criteria will be provided via Blackboard.

FORMAT:

  • 2-to-3-minute short video (VLOG)
  • Not a PowerPoint presentation
  • You must appear in the VLOG, but you do not need to be speaking to the camera the whole time
  • You are welcome to momentarily insert a slide or two of information, include some narrated animation, or add a short video clip to help explain a concept
  • A variety of recording modes can be used: video camera, phone camera, laptop camera, etc
  • Acceptable file formats: .mp4, .avi, .wmv or .mov


TASK Description:

  • Select a compound from a list provided to you
  • Identify and introduce the chemical compound
  • Describe the key properties of the compound and make a link to its structure, a physical 3D visual aid must be shown (examples will be provided for you to use as template)
  • Highlight its relevance in (animal) nutrition
  • Include a list of references
  • A list of approved nutrients/compounds has been uploaded to Blackboard. Choose one compound from the approved list


Acknowledging and/or referencing AI use 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.  

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.  

The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, in Blackboard, via the Turnitin link by the submission deadline. You should also retain an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit. Students are expected to use their own devices to complete this assessment. The assessment is to be completed individually.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 12(10 Aug - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Practical Content

Every Module will have one practical session (repeated in two alternative timeslots)

Module 1: Week 3, Prac 01: Tuesday 9:00 - 12:00; Prac 02: Tuesday 1:00 - 4:00 pm

Module 2: Week 6, Prac 01: Tuesday 9:00 - 12:00; Prac 02: Tuesday 1:00 - 4:00 pm

Module 3: Week 7, 8, 9 (3 weeks in row), Prac 01: Tuesday 9:00 - 12:00; Prac 02: Tuesday 1:00 - 4:00 pm

Module 4: Week 12, Prac 01: Tuesday 9:00 - 12:00; Prac 02: Tuesday 1:00 - 4:00 pm


Sub-activity: Practical 1: - Introduction to feeds and supplements - Common feed ingredients and their major nutrient content - Supplement familiarisation (energy, proteins and minerals) - Sampling and measurements, Proximate analyses, Calculating DM of feeds above Practical 2: - Digestibility - In vitro and in sacco digestibility experiments on pasture, silage and hay, use Daisy in vitro and rumen digestive tracks. Measurement and calculations (class results) excel exercise. Practical 3 : Chicken growth bioassay , a 3-week study of nutrients content on growth performance of broiler chicken/ weekly body weight and feed weighing Practical 4: Feed / Diet formulation
